description: This pipeline processes data by applying expressions to each row, modifying tables based on custom scripts, and removing columns as necessary. The results are then exported and saved in various formats, including CSV/XLSX files, with options for including or excluding specific data, such as edges. The pipeline appears to be designed for handling and analyzing tabular data, potentially generated from image processing tasks, and provides a flexible framework for customizing the output based on user-defined rules and annotations.

4e2f47b3-a065-4515-98d0-07b14f34dbca.name: table-apply-expression-per-row-v2
4e2f47b3-a065-4515-98d0-07b14f34dbca.description: Applies an expression for each row. The column values are available as variables.

02565fdd-b6eb-4f32-87dd-3189cb3bdd18.name: iji-export-table-v2
02565fdd-b6eb-4f32-87dd-3189cb3bdd18.description:
Exports a results table to CSV/XLSX

This algorithm groups the incoming data based on the annotations. Those groups can consist of one data item per slot.

ad46b839-a770-4891-bb66-13b3eb5cd76a.name: modify-tables
ad46b839-a770-4891-bb66-13b3eb5cd76a.description: Executes a Python-script for each table. Each table is passed as dictionary 'table' with the column name as key and values being an array of strings or doubles. The number of input rows can be accessed via the 'nrow' variable.

9e4f18bd-d2da-40e4-8548-c478fdcb379d.name: table-remove-columns
9e4f18bd-d2da-40e4-8548-c478fdcb379d.description: Removes one or multiple columns by name
